ac:rich-text-body.p: The SKA comprises two complementary arrays: SKA-Low for low-frequency observations (50–350 MHz) in Western Australia, and SKA-Mid for mid-frequency observations (0.35–15.4 GHz) in South Africa.
Location: Inyarrimanha Ilgari Bundara, Murchison Radio-Astronomy Observatory, Western Australia | Frequency: 50–350 MHz | Antennas: 131,072 dipoles across 512 stations

tr.td: ~50% of stations within 1 km diameter core
Remaining stations in clusters of 6, logarithmically spaced on 3 spiral arms

ul.li: Input Data Rate: Up to 66 Tb/s

ac:rich-text-body.p: SKA-Low delivers 25% better angular resolution than LOFAR, 8× greater sensitivity, and 135× faster survey speed.
Location: Karoo Radio Astronomy Reserve, Northern Cape, South Africa | Frequency: 0.35–15.4 GHz | Dishes: 133 SKA + 64 MeerKAT (197 total)

tr.td: ~50% of dishes randomly distributed within 2 km core
Remaining dishes logarithmically spaced on 3 spiral arms

ac:rich-text-body.p: SKA-Mid incorporates 64 existing 13.5-metre MeerKAT dishes into the unified array, enabling early science operations while SKA construction continues. The MeerKAT integration provides immediate observational capability and validates combined-array signal processing.
Together, SKA-Low and SKA-Mid provide continuous frequency coverage from 50 MHz to 14 GHz, enabling complementary science across the full SKA portfolio.

p: The SKA processes incoming radio signals through a multi-stage pipeline:

ol.li: Correlation: Raw antenna signals from distributed stations/dishes are cross-correlated to produce visibility data. SKA-Low's correlator generates 500,000 visibilities at processing rates exceeding 10 PetaOps/s.
Science Data Processor (SDP): Visibility data undergoes calibration, flagging, and imaging to produce science-ready products.
Data Products: Output includes continuum images (total-intensity maps), spectral-line cubes (3D intensity maps across frequency), and derived catalogues.
Data Rates: SKA-Low input rates reach 66 Tb/s; output science data is compressed and managed for archival and distribution.

ac:rich-text-body.p: Signal processing represents a significant computational and storage challenge. Real-time processing and data management strategies are essential for SKA operations.

tr.td: Vastly superior survey speed and sensitivity for low-frequency science
Established low-frequency pathfinder; SKA-Low achieves 25% better resolution, 8× sensitivity, 135× survey speed
Next-generation mid-frequency facility with exceptional sensitivity and angular resolution
Proven mid-frequency observatory; SKA-Mid offers 7× more collecting area and wider frequency coverage
Cutting-edge African facility now integrated into SKA-Mid; SKA-Mid adds 133 additional 15 m dishes for enhanced sensitivity

ac:rich-text-body.p: For construction timelines and deployment phases, see the SKA Staged Delivery and Array Assemblies page.
